freshness.cadence: Update when funnel semantics, parity status, or manual counts change.
freshness.confidenceRule: Funnel semantics are locked by CEO decision. Prototype parity is partial parity; downstream stages remain manual where instrumentation or representation is incomplete.

summary.focus: Track the approved BriefLink funnel from visit to account conversion, with current operating priority on post-share progress toward first reply.
summary.sourceOfTruth: CEO-approved funnel semantics with manual operating inputs until instrumentation is live.

sections.title: Approved Funnel Definition
sections.description: Locked product analytics baseline for BriefLink.

items.label: Funnel
items.note: Use this exact five-step sequence for reporting.

items.label: Current parity classification
items.note: Creation and sharing are represented; downstream reply and conversion remain assumed/manual.

items.label: Metric confidence
items.note: Counts remain manual until instrumentation exists; downstream stages may be assumed/manual where prototype parity is incomplete.

sections.title: Core Funnel
sections.description: Manual counters for the approved BriefLink funnel.

metrics.label: visit
metrics.definition: Users who enter the top of the approved funnel.

metrics.label: brief-created
metrics.dependencyNote: Represented in the current prototype; counts remain manual until instrumentation exists.

metrics.label: brief-shared
metrics.definition: Users who share a brief. This is the activation event.
metrics.dependencyNote: Represented in the current prototype; counts remain manual until instrumentation exists.

metrics.label: reply-received
metrics.definition: Shared briefs that receive a reply.
metrics.dependencyNote: Assumed/manual until reply tracking is instrumented and represented through post-share status and first-reply visibility.

metrics.label: account-converted
metrics.definition: Users or accounts that convert after the reply stage.
metrics.dependencyNote: Assumed/manual until conversion tracking is instrumented and represented in the prototype.

sections.title: Parity Breakdown
sections.description: Separate what the current prototype represents from what operations still assumes manually.

columns.label: Represented stages
columns.note: These stages are currently represented in the additive prototype.

columns.label: Assumed stages
columns.note: These stages still rely on manual interpretation, operating review, or future instrumentation.

sections.title: Current Priority Focus
sections.description: Operational focus for the next product/dashboard slice.

items.label: Priority transition
items.note: Primary goal is advancing users from activation into first measurable response.

items.label: Post-share status
items.note: Add explicit post-share state so operators can tell whether a shared brief is awaiting a response, has activity, or remains unresolved.

items.label: First-reply visibility
items.note: Make first reply visible so the team can track movement into reply-received without relying only on offline/manual interpretation.

sections.title: Prototype Parity
sections.description: Tracks which approved funnel stages are currently represented in the additive prototype versus still inferred operationally.

items.label: Parity classification
items.note: The prototype covers creation and sharing, but not the full post-share funnel.

items.label: brief-created
items.note: Current additive prototype includes brief creation flow representation.

items.label: brief-shared
items.note: Current additive prototype includes brief sharing; this is the activation event.

items.label: reply-received
items.note: Not yet instrumented or represented in the current prototype; post-share status and first-reply visibility are the next priority slice.

items.label: account-converted
items.note: Not yet instrumented or represented in the current prototype; track manually if needed.

sections.title: Manual Source of Truth
sections.description: How to maintain this dashboard while instrumentation is pending.

fields.label: Counting method
fields.value: Manual tally from operating review, product reporting, and temporary evidence sources.

fields.label: Update rule
fields.value: Overwrite counts only with the approved five-step funnel: visit, brief-created, brief-shared, reply-received, account-converted.

fields.label: Activation rule
fields.value: Treat brief-shared as the activation event everywhere in this dashboard.

fields.label: Parity rule
fields.value: Classify current state as partial parity until post-share status and first-reply visibility exist for reply-received.

fields.label: Prototype parity caveat
fields.value: reply-received and account-converted remain assumed/manual until they are instrumented or explicitly represented in the prototype.

fields.label: Primary owner
fields.value: vizops maintains the dashboard; product and growth should provide updated manual counts until instrumentation exists.

sections.title: Resolved Funnel Decisions
sections.description: Approved decisions currently reflected in this dashboard.

items.resolution: Use visit -> brief-created -> brief-shared -> reply-received -> account-converted.
items.impact: All reporting now uses one locked five-step funnel.
Activation reporting now uses a stable, approved event.

items.resolution: Treat the current dashboard/prototype state as partial parity.
items.impact: Operational reporting now clearly separates represented stages from assumed stages.

items.resolution: Prioritize post-share status and first-reply visibility to advance from brief-shared into reply-received.
items.impact: The next dashboard/product slice is focused on post-share progress rather than redefining the funnel.

items.resolution: Keep counts manual until instrumentation exists.
items.impact: The dashboard stays operational without inventing unsupported data.
